name: Explain pending QOS and account limits
summary: Measure whether an agent distinguishes dependency, priority, association, QOS, and fairshare signals.
prompt: A user has one pending job with a dependency reason and another with an association group CPU limit. Use the provided queue, job-record, and policy snippets to explain what evidence points to each cause and what public-safe follow-up the user should take.

fixtures.description: Synthetic squeue output for a dependency-blocked job.
Synthetic scontrol job record with dependency fields.
Synthetic squeue output with association group limit reason.
Synthetic visible policy and fairshare evidence.

rubric.description: Separates Dependency from AssocGrpCpuLimit and avoids collapsing both into priority.
Uses account, QOS, Fairshare, GrpTRES, and dependency evidence from fixtures.
Selects both pending-reason and QOS/account limit triage skills when available.
Recommends read-only evidence collection, local policy review, or support handoff.
Does not promise exact start times or scheduler behavior not in evidence.

expected_outcomes: The agent should distinguish scheduler reason classes.
The agent should treat policy and priority fields as evidence, not guarantees.
